# Nidhin Mahesh

> Senior software engineer with 5+ years building blockchain protocols and distributed systems. Co-led development of the Rubix Layer 1 protocol (Proof-of-Pledge consensus, gasless transactions) and a zero-knowledge rollup on zkEVM at Jupiter Meta; shipped production self-custody wallets, staking systems, and payment infrastructure. Author of four peer-reviewed papers on blockchain security and decentralized systems.

## Key Resources

- [Homepage](https://nidhinmahesh.github.io/): Bio, experience timeline, and publications
- [CV (PDF)](https://nidhinmahesh.github.io/nidhinmahesh_CV_.pdf): Full resume
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Vt3x8ukAAAAJ&hl=en): Peer-reviewed publications

## About

Nidhin is a distributed ledger technology specialist with five years of experience building blockchain protocols and infrastructure, including consensus systems, self-custodial wallets, staking, and settlement layers. He currently builds Fexr, an independent Web3 infrastructure platform that evolved from a single chain custody solution into a multichain settlement layer with agentic trading engines and smart contract integrations. Previously, as Lead Engineer at Jupiter Meta, he was a founding engineering team member for the JMDT blockchain, a zero-knowledge rollup on zkEVM. Before that, as Co-Lead on the Rubix Layer 1 protocol, he helped develop the zero-gas-fee Proof-of-Pledge network from testnet through mainnet and led the full migration of the protocol stack from Spring Boot to Golang.

His research spans cyber security and decentralized systems, with four peer-reviewed papers published across IEEE COMSNETS, Springer ICTIS, and ACM workshops at SenSys. At Amrita University, he led the Google Developer Student Club and contributed to the FOSS club, ranked in the top 10 nationally for Google Summer of Code selections. He now works at the intersection of engineering and go-to-market strategy, helping teams evaluate, integrate, and adopt digital asset infrastructure.

## Skills

- Languages: Golang, Rust, Solidity, Java, Python, TypeScript, Dart
- Blockchain: L1 protocol design, ZK rollups / zkEVM, consensus (Proof-of-Pledge), smart contracts, self-custody wallets, P2P networking, Hyperledger Fabric, IPFS, tokenomics, DeFi
- Platform: Spring Boot, REST API design, FastAPI, Next.js, Flutter, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, microservices

## Experience

- Full-Stack Developer (Indie Project), Fexr · Self-employed — Dubai, UAE · Remote (Sep 2025 – Present). Architected and deployed a production-grade Web3 infrastructure platform, scaling it from a single-chain custody solution to a multi-chain settlement layer; engineered agentic trading engines and smart-contract systems while owning the full cloud-native deployment pipeline solo.
- Lead Engineer, Jupiter Meta — Hyderabad, India (Nov 2024 – Aug 2025). Founding engineering team member; contributed to the design and launch of the JMDT blockchain, an in-house zero-knowledge rollup on zkEVM. Designed ZK circuit architectures and proof-verification pipelines.
- Lead Engineer — Rubix Layer 1 Blockchain Protocol, Rubix Network — Hyderabad, India (Mar 2021 – Nov 2024). Coordinated the engineering team behind testnet and mainnet infrastructure; delivered the network's first native wallet with Decentralized Identifier (DID) support; built core protocol services (P2P networking, transaction processing, Proof-of-Pledge consensus, asset custody); led the full migration from Spring Boot to Golang (2023).
- Research Assistant, TIFAC-CORE in Cyber Security — Coimbatore, India (Jan 2020 – Apr 2020). Developed a secure, permissioned blockchain architecture for immutable data provenance and audit trails for a national defense organization.
- Research Intern, Amrita Center for Wireless Networks & Applications — Kollam, India (Jun 2019 – Sep 2019). Built a Hyperledger blockchain test-bed of 7 Raspberry Pi nodes running Golang smart contracts; validated P2P energy-trading workflows on constrained IoT hardware.
- Writer, Towards Data Science · Freelance — Remote (Dec 2017 – May 2018). Authored machine-learning tutorials and technical guides on TensorFlow.

## Publications

- "Smartverse: Blockchain-Based Crowdsourced V2X Message Verification and Dissemination System" — 15th Int'l Conference on Communication Systems & Networks (COMSNETS), 2023 — https://doi.org/10.1109/COMSNETS56262.2023.10041363
- "Blockchain Framework for Social Media DRM Based on Secret Sharing" — Springer, Int'l Conference on ICT for Intelligent Systems (ICTIS), 2021
- "Conceptualizing Blockchain-Based Energy Market for Self-Sustainable Community" — ACM BlockSys Workshop @ SenSys, New York, 2019
- "Decentralized Power System and Future Mobility: The Use Cases of Community-Driven Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure" — ACM Int'l Workshop on Smart Cities and Communities (TESCA), 2019

## Education

- B.Tech, Computer Science & Engineering, Amrita School of Engineering, Amritapuri (2016 – 2020)
- Led the Google Developer Student Club and contributed through the FOSS club — ranked top-10 nationally for Google Summer of Code selections
